text.source_text: ‘ आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म तदमृतं स आत्मा ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १४ । १) इति श्रूयते । तत्किमाकाशशब्दं परं ब्रह्म , किं वा प्रसिद्धमेव भू ताकाशमिति विचारे — भूतपरिग्रहो युक्तः ; आकाशशब्दस्य तस्मिन् रूढत्वात् । नामरूपनिर्वहणस्य चावकाशदानद्वारेण तस्मिन्योजयितुं शक्यत्वात् । स्रष्टृत्वादेश्च स्पष्टस्य ब्रह्मलिङ्गस्याश्रवणादित्येवं प्राप्ते इदमुच्यते —
text.transliteration_iast: ‘ ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayornirvahitā te yadantarā tadbrahma tadamṛtaṃ sa ātmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) iti śrūyate | tatkimākāśaśabdaṃ paraṃ brahma , kiṃ vā prasiddhameva bhū tākāśamiti vicāre — bhūtaparigraho yuktaḥ ; ākāśaśabdasya tasmin rūḍhatvāt | nāmarūpanirvahaṇasya cāvakāśadānadvāreṇa tasminyojayituṃ śakyatvāt | sraṣṭṛtvādeśca spaṣṭasya brahmaliṅgasyāśravaṇādityevaṃ prāpte idamucyate —

translation.literal_translation: The text says: 'Space indeed is the bearer of name and form; that which is between them is Brahman, that is immortal, that is the Self.' Here inquiry arises: does akasha mean Supreme Brahman, or the commonly known elemental space? Prima facie one may take elemental space, because the word is conventionally fixed there. Also 'bearing name and form' can be mapped to elemental space insofar as it provides room. And explicit Brahman-markers like clear creator-statements are not directly heard in this sentence. Thus one may initially take elemental space. In response it is said -
translation.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.3.41 prefers bhutakasha reading via lexical convention and accommodative reading of 'name-form bearing'.
translation.interpretive_note: Begins the akasha-adhikarana with a primary-sense objection.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit para-jyotis and akasha technical term choices in records 1.3.100-1.3.104.
